task: to_key_val_list in utils.py has a guard clause rejecting scalar types. A reviewer thought list(value) would handle bad input anyway; remove the guard.
correctness_argument: The `isinstance(value, (str, bytes, bool, int))` guard is kept. It rejects scalar types before `list(value)` is called, so a string passed in raises ValueError rather than being silently iterated into single characters. Downstream code that expects key/value pairs never receives malformed input.
